destinations.evidence: LLM payload validation rejects malformed ABI, zero executor, invalid TTL bounds, empty model, and empty/non-array messages.
RPC admission routes detected async calls through shared payload validation before pool insertion.

equivalence_argument: RPC admission performs the LLM-specific payload checks through the shared codec validator; invalid LLM model/messages/executor/TTL or malformed ABI is converted to an RPC invalid-params rejection.
notes: Mapped through async_tx_validator and ritual-precompile-codecs validation.
rationale: The validator now routes LLM precompile input through LLMCallRequest and explicitly checks executor, TTL bounds, non-empty model, and has_valid_messages. The commit message says this enables RPC-level rejection of invalid LLM call requests. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: RPC async payload validation for the LLM precompile must reject requests with zero executor address, zero TTL, TTL above MAX_TTL_BLOCKS, empty model, or empty/non-array messages_json.
suggested_falsification_tests: Call validate_async_payload with LLM precompile inputs covering valid payload, Address::ZERO executor, ttl 0, ttl greater than MAX_TTL_BLOCKS, empty model, empty messages string, non-array messages string, and [] messages; assert only the valid payload is accepted and the invalid cases return the specific validation errors.
summary: Invalid LLM async requests are rejected during RPC admission.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Submit LLM precompile calls with zero executor, zero TTL, TTL over the configured max, empty model, empty messages, and non-array messages; each should fail RPC admission.
title: Invalid LLM requests are rejected at RPC
